finding: Prefix-substring rewrite verified against real Postgres on the build cluster: io.github.phibya/* -> io.github.ziee-ai/*, non-phibya reverse-DNS + already-ziee-ai + unknown-slug rows all left untouched, and a second run is a no-op (idempotent).

WHERE hub_id LIKE 'io.github.phibya/%' is the idempotency guard; rewritten rows start with io.github.ziee-ai/ and cannot re-match. hub_entities.hub_id is the sole raw-catalog-id column (migrations 8/69/79/80/92 + repository/handlers confirm), so no other table needs rewriting. Mirrors migration 92's intent.

Considered: the rewrite could theoretically collide with the partial unique indexes uniq_hub_template_install / uniq_hub_system_mcp_install if a deployment already held an io.github.ziee-ai/* template|system row before this migration. Impossible in practice — the io.github.ziee-ai catalog ships WITH this migration, so no ziee-ai rows can pre-exist at migration time; and migration 92 (the direct precedent) is likewise unguarded against slug collisions.

No injection surface: the migration is static SQL with a fixed prefix swap. is_safe_name permits '-' in the namespace bytes, so the rebranded io.github.ziee-ai/* names pass validation, catalog fetch, and the leaf-derived MCP slug regex (leaves unchanged).
DO block uses GET DIAGNOSTICS + RAISE NOTICE only; introduces no new failure path. A zero-row match (deployment never installed a phibya entity) is a silent no-op, which is correct.
Runs inside the single sqlx migration transaction at boot; no new concurrent-access surface.

Catalog `name` + `manifest_path` now io.github.ziee-ai; the manifest endpoint URL-encodes the name (catalog_v1 test updated to io.github.ziee-ai%2F...). No OpenAPI request/response type changed, so no types.ts/openapi.json regen.

Migration mirrors 92's guarded-idempotent shape (technique differs: prefix-substring vs VALUES-map, appropriate for a 1:1 prefix swap). Seed layout <cat>/<ns>/<leaf>/<ver>.json and the test-edit style conform to existing conventions. Fixed a stale 'migration 89' doc typo in the test header.

New migration-131 tests run the real migration SQL on real inserted rows (no behavior mocking): rewrite+idempotency + a 92->131 composition test. The pre-existing 92-isolation tests correctly still assert io.github.phibya (92's true output) rather than being blind-seded.

hub_version kept at 2.0.0 (publisher rename is content, not schema), preserving the seed_index_version_matches_const lockstep with the untouched SEED_HUB_VERSION const. Fresh build-pages.py run confirms 12 ziee-ai items / 0 phibya at hub_version 2.0.0.
Only machine identifiers changed; display_name/description/instructions (user-facing copy) are byte-identical to the canonical hub build output.

binaries/hub-seed (gitignored) regenerates from resources/ via build_helper/hub_seed.rs, so include_dir! bakes the ziee-ai paths; hub_manager get_file tests retargeted to match. Lib+bin and the integration_tests target both compile clean (only a pre-existing unrelated dead-code warning).

Pre-existing (base-reproduced): 5 catalog-read tests granted hub::models::read but /hub/{version,index,manifest} require HubCatalogRead -> 403. Fixed to hub::catalog::read, matching the handler contract + the sibling catalog_read_cannot_refresh test. Scope-limited; refresh/installed grants left as hub::models::read.

Pre-existing (base-reproduced): SEED_ITEM_COUNT=28 and workflows=9 were stale vs the actual 29-item / 10-workflow seed (io.github.ziee/sr-review added upstream). Bumped to 29 / 10. Not caused by the publisher rename (base has the same 29-item seed).
